heading: Resolution Funding Corporation established
label: 1441b Resolution Funding Corporation established

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Resolution Funding Corporation is to provide funds to the Resolution Trust Corporation to enable the Resolution Trust Corporation to carry out the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: There is established a corporation to be known as the Resolution Funding Corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: The director of the Office of Finance of the Federal Home Loan Banks (or the head of any successor office).

children.label: B
children.text: 2 members selected by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board from among the presidents of the Federal Home Loan Banks.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: 2 Terms
children.text: Of the 2 members appointed under paragraph (1)(B), 1 shall be appointed for an initial term of 2 years and 1 shall be appointed for an initial term of 3 years. Thereafter, such members shall be appointed for a term of 3 years.

children.label: A
children.text: such member’s service on the Directorate shall terminate on the date such member leaves such office; and

children.label: B
children.text: the successor to the office of such member shall serve the remainder of such member’s term.

children.heading: Equal representation of banks
children.label: 4 Equal representation of banks
children.text: No president of a Federal Home Loan Bank may be appointed to serve an additional term on the Directorate until such time as the presidents of each of the other Federal Home Loan Banks have served as many terms as the president of such bank.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 5 Chairperson
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall select the chairperson of the Directorate from among the 3 members of the Directorate.

children.heading: No paid employees
children.label: A No paid employees
children.text: The Funding Corporation shall have no paid employees.

children.heading: Powers
children.label: B Powers
children.text: The Directorate may, with the approval of the Director authorize the officers, employees, or agents of the Federal Home Loan Banks to act for and on behalf of the Funding Corporation in such manner as may be necessary to carry out the functions of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: All administrative expenses of the Funding Corporation, including custodian fees, shall be paid by the Federal Home Loan Banks.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate amount the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board required such bank to invest in the Funding Corporation (as of the time of such determination) under paragraphs (4) and (5) of subsection (e) (computed without regard to paragraphs (3) or (6) of such subsection); by

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board required all Federal Home Loan Banks to invest (as of the time of such determination) under such paragraphs.

children.heading: Regulation by Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board
children.label: 8 Regulation by Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board
children.text: The Directorate of the Funding Corporation shall be subject to such regulations, orders, and directions as the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board may prescribe.

children.heading: No compensation from Funding Corporation
children.label: 9 No compensation from Funding Corporation
children.text: Members of the Directorate of the Funding Corporation shall receive no pay, allowance, or benefit from the Funding Corporation for serving on the Directorate.

children.heading: Issue stock
children.label: 1 Issue stock
children.text: To issue nonvoting capital stock to the Federal Home Loan Banks.

children.heading: Purchase capital stock; transfer amounts
children.label: 2 Purchase capital stock; transfer amounts
children.text: To purchase capital certificates issued by the Resolution Trust Corporation under , and to transfer amounts to the Resolution Trust Corporation pursuant to subsection (e)(8) of this section.section 1441a of this title

children.heading: Issue obligations
children.label: 3 Issue obligations
children.text: To issue debentures, bonds, or other obligations, and to borrow, to give security for any amount borrowed, and to pay interest on (and any redemption premium with respect to) any such obligation or amount.

children.heading: Impose assessments
children.label: 4 Impose assessments
children.text: To impose assessments in accordance with subsection (e)(7).

children.heading: Corporate seal
children.label: 5 Corporate seal
children.text: To adopt, alter, and use a corporate seal.

children.heading: Succession
children.label: 6 Succession
children.text: To have succession until dissolved.

children.heading: Contracts
children.label: 7 Contracts
children.text: To enter into contracts.

children.heading: Authority to sue
children.label: 8 Authority to sue
children.text: To sue and be sued in its corporate capacity, and to complain and defend in any action brought by or against the Funding Corporation in any State or Federal court of competent jurisdiction.

children.heading: Incidental powers
children.label: 9 Incidental powers
children.text: To exercise such incidental powers not inconsistent with the provisions of this section and as are necessary and appropriate to carry out the provisions of this section.section 1441a of this title

children.heading: Capitalization of Funding Corporation, etc.
children.label: e Capitalization of Funding Corporation, etc.
i
children.text: the Funding Corporation may transfer the amounts required under paragraph (8); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total of the face amounts (the amount of principal payable at maturity) of noninterest bearing instruments in the Funding Corporation Principal Fund are equal to the aggregate amount of principal on the obligations of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: Purchases of stock by Federal Home Loan Banks
children.label: B Purchases of stock by Federal Home Loan Banks
children.text: Each Federal Home Loan Bank shall purchase stock in the Funding Corporation at times and in amounts prescribed by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board.

children.heading: Par value; transferability
children.label: 2 Par value; transferability
children.text: Each share of stock issued by the Funding Corporation to a Federal Home Loan Bank shall have a par value in an amount determined by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board and shall be transferable at not less than par value only among the Federal Home Loan Banks in the manner and to the extent prescribed by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board.

children.heading: Maximum investment amount limitation for each Federal Home Loan Bank
children.label: 3 Maximum investment amount limitation for each Federal Home Loan Bank

children.heading: Reserves and undivided profits onDecember 31, 1988
children.label: A Reserves and undivided profits onDecember 31, 1988
i
children.text: the reserves maintained by such Bank pursuant to the reserve requirement contained in the first 2 sentences of (as in effect on ); andsection 1436 of this titleDecember 31, 1988

children.label: ii
children.text: the undivided profits of such Bank, minus the amounts invested in the capital stock of the Financing Corporation pursuant to .section 1441 of this title

children.heading: Subsequent additions to reserves and un­divided profits
children.label: B Subsequent additions to reserves and un­divided profits
I
children.text: the amounts added to reserves by such Bank after , pursuant to the reserve requirement contained in the first 2 sentences of (as in effect on ); andDecember 31, 1988December 31, 1988section 1436 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the quarterly additions to undivided profits of the Bank after ; minusDecember 31, 1988

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts invested by such Bank in the capital stock of the Financing Corporation after , pursuant to the requirement contained in .December 31, 1988section 1441 of this title

children.heading: Increase in limit
children.label: i Increase in limit
children.text: If the aggregate amount for all Federal Home Loan Banks determined under subparagraph (B)(i) is less than $300,000,000 per year, the limit for each Bank shall be increased by an amount determined by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board by multiplying the aggregate deficiency by the percentage applicable to such Bank arrived at in the manner described in paragraph (5).

children.heading: Decrease in limit
children.label: ii Decrease in limit
children.text: If the aggregate amount for all Federal Home Loan Banks determined under subparagraph (B)(i) is more than $300,000,000 per year, the limit for each Bank shall be decreased by an amount determined by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board by multiplying the aggregate excess by the percentage applicable to such Bank arrived at in the manner described in paragraph (5).

children.heading: Pro rata distribution of first $1,000,000,000 invested in Funding Corporation by Federal Home Loan Banks
children.label: 4 Pro rata distribution of first $1,000,000,000 invested in Funding Corporation by Federal Home Loan Banks
children.text: Of the first $1,000,000,000 of the aggregate that the Director (pursuant to ) or the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board (under this section) may require the Federal Home Loan Banks collectively to invest in the capital stock of the Financing Corporation or invest in the capital stock of the Funding Corporation, respectively, the amount which each Federal Home Loan Bank (or any successor to the Bank) shall invest shall be determined by the Director or the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board (as the case may be) by multiplying the aggregate amount of such investment by all Banks by the percentage appearing in the following table for each such Bank:section 1441 of this titleBankPercentageFederal Home Loan Bank of Boston1.8629Federal Home Loan Bank of New York9.1006Federal Home Loan Bank of Pittsburgh4.2702Federal Home Loan Bank of Atlanta14.4007Federal Home Loan Bank of Cincinnati8.2653Federal Home Loan Bank of Indianapolis5.2863Federal Home Loan Bank of Chicago9.6886Federal Home Loan Bank of Des Moines6.9301Federal Home Loan Bank of Dallas8.8181Federal Home Loan Bank of Topeka5.2706Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco19.9644Federal Home Loan Bank of Seattle6.1422

children.heading: Pro rata distribution of amounts required to be invested in excess of $1,000,000,000
children.label: 5 Pro rata distribution of amounts required to be invested in excess of $1,000,000,000
A
children.text: the sum of the total assets (as of the most recent December 31) held by all Savings Association Insurance Fund members as of the date of funding which are members of such Bank; by

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the total assets (as of such date) held by all Savings Association Insurance Fund members as of the date of funding which are members of a Federal Home Loan Bank.

children.heading: Special provisions relating to maximum amount limitations
children.label: 6 Special provisions relating to maximum amount limitations
i
children.text: the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall require each Federal Home Loan Bank that is not allocated an amount under paragraph (5) that exceeds its maximum under paragraph (3) (in this paragraph referred to as a “remaining Bank”) to purchase stock in the Funding Corporation (in addition to the amount determined under paragraph (5) for such remaining Bank and subject to the maximum amount applicable with respect to such remaining Bank under paragraph (3) at the time of such determination) on behalf of the deficient Bank the amount determined under subparagraph (B);

children.label: ii
children.text: the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall require the deficient Bank to subsequently reimburse the remaining Banks out of its net earnings (or reimbursements received from other Banks) in the manner described in subparagraphs (C) and (D); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the requirements contained in subparagraph (D) relating to the use of net earnings shall apply to the deficient Bank until such Bank has reimbursed the remaining Banks for all of the excess amount.

children.heading: Allocation of excess amount among remaining Federal Home Loan Banks
children.label: B Allocation of excess amount among remaining Federal Home Loan Banks
I
children.text: the cumulative amount of stock in the Funding Corporation purchased under this subsection by such remaining Bank at the time of such determination; by

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate of the cumulative amounts invested under this subsection by all remaining Banks at such time.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: ii Reallocation
children.text: If the allocation under this subparagraph results in a remaining Bank exceeding its maximum amount under paragraph (3), such excess amount shall be reallocated to the other remaining Bank in accordance with this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A Bank on whose behalf stock is purchased under subparagraph (A)(i) shall make payments annually from amounts, if any, in its reserve account (as described in subparagraph (D)) to each Bank that made payments on its behalf until a full reimbursement has been completed. A full reimbursement shall require repayment of the excess amounts invested by other Banks plus interest which shall accrue at a rate equal to the annual average cost of funds in the most recent year to all Federal Home Loan Banks and which shall begin to accrue 2 years after the investments under subparagraph (A)(i) are made.

children.heading: Determination of amounts
children.label: ii Determination of amounts
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall annually determine the dollar amounts of such reimbursements by distributing the amount available for such reimbursements (at the time of such determination) from the reimbursing Bank to the Banks that made purchases on its behalf according to the shares of the reimbursing Bank’s excess amount that the other Banks invested.

children.heading: Transfer to account for reimbursements required
children.label: D Transfer to account for reimbursements required

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Of the net earnings for any year of a Bank on whose behalf a purchase is made under subparagraph (A)(i) and any reimbursements received from other Banks, the amount necessary to make the reimbursements required under subparagraph (A)(ii) shall be placed in a reserve account (established in the manner prescribed by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board), which shall be available only for such reimbursements.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The total amount placed in such reserve account in any year by any Bank shall not exceed an amount equal to 20 percent of the net earnings of such Bank for such year.

children.heading: Issuance
children.label: 1 Issuance
children.text: The Funding Corporation may issue bonds, notes, debentures, and similar obligations in an aggregate amount not to exceed $30,000,000,000. No obligation may be issued under this paragraph unless, at the time of issuance, the face amounts (the amount of principal payable at maturity) of noninterest bearing instruments in the Funding Corporation Principal Fund are equal to the aggregate amount of principal on the obligations of the Funding Corporation that will be outstanding following such issuance.

children.heading: Earnings on certain assets
children.label: A Earnings on certain assets
children.text: Earnings on assets of the Funding Corporation which are not invested in the Funding Corporation Principal Fund shall be used for interest payments on outstanding debt of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: Proceeds from Resolution Trust Corporation
children.label: B Proceeds from Resolution Trust Corporation
i
children.text: the liquidating dividends and payments made on claims received by the Resolution Trust Corporation from receiverships to the extent such proceeds are determined by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board to be in excess of funds presently necessary for resolution costs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any proceeds from warrants and participations acquired by the Resolution Trust Corporation.

children.heading: Payments by Federal home loan banks
children.label: C Payments by Federal home loan banks

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To the extent that the amounts available pursuant to subparagraphs (A) and (B) are insufficient to cover the amount of interest payments, each Federal home loan bank shall pay to the Funding Corporation in each calendar year, 20.0 percent of the net earnings of that Bank (after deducting expenses relating to and operating expenses).section 1430(j) of this title

children.heading: Annual determination
children.label: ii Annual determination
children.text: The Director annually shall determine the extent to which the value of the aggregate amounts paid by the Federal home loan banks exceeds or falls short of the value of an annuity of $300,000,000 per year that commences on the issuance date and ends on the final scheduled maturity date of the obligations, and shall select appropriate present value factors for making such determinations, in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Payment term alterations
children.label: iii Payment term alterations
children.text: The Director shall extend or shorten the term of the payment obligations of a Federal home loan bank under this subparagraph as necessary to ensure that the value of all payments made by the Banks is equivalent to the value of an annuity referred to in clause (ii).

children.heading: Term beyond maturity
children.label: iv Term beyond maturity
children.text: If the Director extends the term of payment obligations beyond the final scheduled maturity date for the obligations, each Federal home loan bank shall continue to pay 20.0 percent of its net earnings (after deducting expenses relating to and operating expenses) to the Treasury of the United States until the value of all such payments by the Federal home loan banks is equivalent to the value of an annuity referred to in clause (ii). In the final year in which the Federal home loan banks are required to make any payment to the Treasury under this subparagraph, if the dollar amount represented by 20.0 percent of the net earnings of the Federal home loan banks exceeds the remaining obligation of the Banks to the Treasury, the Director shall reduce the percentage pro rata to a level sufficient to pay the remaining obligation.section 1430(j) of this title

children.heading: Semiannual reports
children.label: v Semiannual reports
children.text: The Director shall report semiannually to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives on the projected date for the completion of contributions required by this section.

children.heading: Proceeds from sale of assets
children.label: D Proceeds from sale of assets
children.text: To the extent the amounts available pursuant to subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) are insufficient to cover the amount of interest payments, the FSLIC Resolution Fund shall transfer to the Funding Corporation any net proceeds from the sale of assets received from the Resolution Trust Corporation, which shall be used by the Funding Corporation to pay such interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To the extent the amounts available pursuant to subparagraphs (A), (B), (C), and (D) are insufficient to cover the amount of interest payments, the Secretary of the Treasury shall pay to the Funding Corporation the additional amount due, which shall be used by the Funding Corporation to pay such interest.

children.heading: Liability of Funding Corporation
children.label: ii Liability of Funding Corporation
children.text: In each instance where the Secretary is required to make a payment under this subparagraph to the Funding Corporation, the amount of the payment shall become a liability of the Funding Corporation to be repaid to the Secretary upon dissolution of the Funding Corporation (to the extent the Funding Corporation may have any remaining assets).

children.heading: Appropriation of funds
children.label: iii Appropriation of funds
children.text: There are hereby appropriated to the Secretary, for fiscal year 1989 and each fiscal year thereafter, such sums as may be necessary to carry out clause (i).

children.heading: Principal payments
children.label: 3 Principal payments
children.text: On maturity of an obligation issued under this subsection, the obligation shall be repaid by the Funding Corporation from the liquidation of noninterest bearing instruments held in the Funding Corporation Principal Fund.

children.heading: Proceeds to be transferred to Resolution Trust Corporation
children.label: 4 Proceeds to be transferred to Resolution Trust Corporation
A
children.text: purchase the capital certificates issued by the Resolution Trust Corporation under ; orsection 1441a of this title

children.label: B
children.text: refund any previously issued obligation the proceeds of which were transferred in the manner described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Investment of United States funds in obligations
children.label: 5 Investment of United States funds in obligations
children.text: Obligations issued under this section by the Funding Corporation, at the direction of the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall be lawful investments, and may be accepted as security, for all fiduciary, trust, and public funds the investment or deposit of which shall be under the authority or control of the United States or any officer of the United States.

children.heading: Market for obligations
children.label: 6 Market for obligations
children.text: All persons having the power to invest in, sell, underwrite, purchase for their own accounts, accept as security, or otherwise deal in obligations of the Federal Home Loan Banks shall also have the power to do so with respect to obligations of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), obligations of the Funding Corporation shall be exempt from tax both as to principal and interest to the same extent as any obligation of a Federal Home Loan Bank is exempt from tax under .section 1433 of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Funding Corporation, like the Federal Home Loan Banks, shall be treated as an agency of the United States for purposes of the first sentence of (relating to determination of tax status of interest on obligations).section 3124(b) of title 31

children.label: i
children.text: shall not be considered to be securities issued or guaranteed by a person controlled or supervised by, or acting as an instrumentality of, the Government of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be considered to be “exempted securities” within the meaning of , except that such obligations shall be considered to be exempted securities for purposes of section 78 of title 15.section 78c(a)(12)(A)(i) of title 15o

children.heading: Authority of Commission
children.label: B Authority of Commission
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the Securities and Exchange Commission may, by rule or order, consistent with the public interest and the protection of investors, exempt securities issued by the Funding Corporation from the registration requirements of the Securities Act of 1933 [ et seq.], subject to such terms and conditions as the Commission may prescribe.15 U.S.C. 77a

children.heading: Minority participation in public or negotiated offerings
children.label: 9 Minority participation in public or negotiated offerings
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board and the Directorate shall ensure that minority owned or controlled commercial banks, investment banking firms, underwriters, and bond counsels throughout the United States have an opportunity to participate to a significant degree in any public or negotiated offering of obligations issued under this section.

children.heading: No full faith and credit of the United States
children.label: 10 No full faith and credit of the United States
children.text: Obligations of the Funding Corporation shall not be obligations of, or guaranteed as to principal by, the Federal Home Loan Bank System, the Federal Home Loan Banks, the United States, or the Resolution Trust Corporation and the obligations shall so plainly state. The Secretary shall pay interest on such obligations as required pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: Use and disposition of assets of Funding Corporation not transferred to Resolution Trust Corporation
children.label: g Use and disposition of assets of Funding Corporation not transferred to Resolution Trust Corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to regulations, restrictions, and limitations prescribed by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board, assets of the Funding Corporation which are not required to be invested in capital certificates issued by the Resolution Trust Corporation under and are not needed for current interest payments shall be invested in direct obligations of the United States issued by the Secretary.section 1441a of this title

children.heading: Separate account for zero coupon instruments held to ensure payment of principal
children.label: 2 Separate account for zero coupon instruments held to ensure payment of principal
A
children.text: which are direct obligations of the United States issued by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total of the face amounts (the amount of principal payable at maturity) of which is approximately equal to the aggregate amount of principal on the obligations of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: Treatment for certain purposes
children.label: 1 Treatment for certain purposes
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (f)(7)(B), the Funding Corporation shall be treated as a Federal Home Loan Bank for purposes of (to the extent such section relates to State, municipal, and local taxation) and .section 1433 of this titlesection 1443 of this title

children.heading: Federal Reserve banks as depositaries and fiscal agents
children.label: 2 Federal Reserve banks as depositaries and fiscal agents
children.text: The Federal Reserve banks are authorized to act as depositaries for or fiscal agents or custodians of the Funding Corporation.

children.heading: Applicability of certain provisions relating to Government corporations
children.label: 3 Applicability of certain provisions relating to Government corporations
children.text: The Funding Corporation shall be treated, for purposes of sections 9105, 9107, and 9108 of title 31, as a mixed-ownership Government corporation which has capital of the Government.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Federal court jurisdiction
children.label: A Federal court jurisdiction
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any civil action, suit, or proceeding to which the Funding Corporation is a party shall be deemed to arise under the laws of the United States, and the United States district courts shall have original jurisdiction over such action, suit, or proceeding.

children.heading: Removal
children.label: B Removal
children.text: The Funding Corporation may, without bond or security, remove any such action, suit, or proceeding from a State court to the United States District Court for the District of Columbia.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall annually submit a full report of the operations, activities, budget, receipts, and expenditures of the Funding Corporation for the preceding 12-month period.

children.label: A
children.text: audited statements and any information necessary to make known the financial condition and operations of the Funding Corporation in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles;

children.label: B
children.text: the financial operating plans and forecasts (including estimates of actual and future spending, and estimates of actual and future cash obligations) of the Funding Corporation taking into account its financial commitments, guarantees, and other contingent liabilities; and

children.label: C
children.text: the results of the annual audit of the financial transactions of the Funding Corporation conducted by the Comptroller General pursuant to .section 9105(a) of title 31

children.heading: Submission to Congress and President
children.label: 3 Submission to Congress and President
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board shall submit each annual report required under this subsection to the Congress and the President as soon as practicable after the end of the calendar year for which the report is made, but not later than June 30 of the year following such calendar year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Funding Corporation shall be dissolved, as soon as practicable, after the maturity and full payment of all obligations issued by the Funding Corporation under this section.

children.heading: Authority of Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board to conclude affairs of Funding Corporation
children.label: 2 Authority of Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board to conclude affairs of Funding Corporation
children.text: Effective on the date of the dissolution of the Funding Corporation under paragraph (1), the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board may exercise on behalf of the Funding Corporation any power of the Funding Corporation which the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board determines to be necessary to settle and conclude the affairs of the Funding Corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: any interest on, or any redemption premium with respect to, any obligation of the Funding Corporation; or

children.label: B
children.text: issuance costs.

children.label: A
children.text: any fee incurred by the Funding Corporation in connection with the transfer of any security to, or the maintenance of any security in, the segregated account established under subsection (g); and

children.label: B
children.text: any other expense incurred by the Funding Corporation in connection with the establishment or maintenance of such account.

children.heading: Funding Corporation
children.label: 3 Funding Corporation
children.text: The term “Funding Corporation” means the Resolution Funding Corporation established in subsection (b).

children.heading: Funding Corporation Principal Fund
children.label: 4 Funding Corporation Principal Fund
children.text: The term “Funding Corporation Principal Fund” means the separate account established under subsection (g)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: means issuance fees and commissions incurred by the Funding Corporation in connection with the issuance or servicing of any obligation of the Funding Corporation; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes legal and accounting expenses, trustee and fiscal and paying agent charges, costs incurred in connection with preparing and printing offering materials, and advertising expenses, to the extent that any such cost or expense is incurred by the Funding Corporation in connection with issuing any obligation.

children.heading: Net earnings
children.label: 6 Net earnings
children.text: The term “net earnings” means net earnings without reduction for chargeoffs or expenses incurred by a Federal Home Loan Bank for the purchase of capital stock of the Financing Corporation or payments relating to the Funding Corporation required by the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board under subsections (e) and (f).

children.label: A
children.text: the Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board of the Resolution Trust Corporation under ; andsection 1441a of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System; and2See 2008 Amendment note below.2

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: A
children.text: that portion required to be added to reserves maintained pursuant to the first 2 sentences of ; andsection 1436 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the dollar amounts held by the respective Federal Home Loan Banks in special dividend stabilization reserves on , as determined by the table set forth in .December 31, 1985section 1441(d)(7) of this title

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: l Regulations
children.text: The Thrift Depositor Protection Oversight Board may prescribe any regulations necessary to carry out this section.
